mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% in 3 months signals a weakening labor market that reduces corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening demand cycle that boosts corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the housing market index below 1000 in 2 months signals a weakening supply cycle that reduces corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining housing market index are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% in 3 months and a decline in industrial production below 100 in 2 months signal a weakening labor market and a strengthening demand cycle that reduces corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 4.5% in 3 months and INDPRO < 100 in 2 months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate and declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in real GDP signals a strengthening demand cycle that boosts corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% in 3 months signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the housing market index below 1000 in 2 months and a decline in industrial production below 100 in 2 months signal a weakening supply cycle and a strengthening demand cycle that reduces corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1000 in 2 months and INDPRO < 100 in 2 months
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining housing market index and declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
